tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: At 40, Vozinha Stuns Spain With Brilliant Saves—Then Gets the Best Goal of All: His Mom's Visa
stories.summary: Cape Verde's 40-year-old goalkeeper delivers seven stunning saves, then tearfully celebrates as U.S. State Department helps reunite him with his mother.

stories.headline: Cancer Survivor's Friendship With UNC Baseball Team Carries Him to the College World Series
stories.summary: Six-year-old leukemia survivor David, visited by UNC baseball players during chemo treatments, cheers the Tar Heels at the College World Series in Omaha.

stories.headline: Lost Engagement Ring Found Down a Toilet, Returned After 10 Years—Sparkling and Restored
stories.summary: Carol Oram's engagement ring, lost down a toilet 10 years ago, sparkles again after plumbers discover it and a jeweler polishes it to like-new beauty.

stories.headline: Man Living With HIV Receives Life-Saving Transplant From HIV-Positive Donor in Historic First
stories.summary: Bertrand Nelson, living with HIV for over 20 years, receives historic double lung and liver transplant from HIV-positive donor on March 21, opening doors for thousands.

stories.headline: At 96, Osvaldo Still Shows Up to Serve—Inspiring His Whole Family to Give Back
stories.summary: Osvaldo Figueroa, 96, celebrated his June 20 birthday volunteering at Mother Cabrini Food Pantry, where he's served from its earliest days nearly two decades ago.

stories.headline: Nine Sea Turtles Return to the Ocean After Months of Loving Rehabilitation Care
stories.summary: Nine sea turtles named after Baltimore neighborhoods—St. Paul, Fells Point, Glen, and others—swam joyfully back into the Atlantic after months of rehabilitation.

stories.headline: Down 7 Runs in the 9th, A's Complete Thrilling Comeback With Pinch-Hitter's Two-Run Homer
stories.summary: Jonah Heim's two-run homer in the ninth tied it as the A's rallied from 7 runs down to beat the Angels 12-11 in 10 innings of pure baseball magic.

stories.headline: Teen's Love for One Hat Blooms Into Cozy Hearts Collective, Knitting Warmth for Newborns
stories.summary: Ryan Musyoka connected local knitters to create at least 80 handmade hats for babies at his hospital, inspired by a single gift.

stories.headline: Ultra-Runner Sprints 566 Kilometers Across Sri Lanka, Raises $27,000 for Children Fighting Cancer
stories.summary: Summary unchanged: excerpt contains no usable details beyond the headline information.

stories.headline: Lake Tuz's Flamingos Surge Back: 5,000 Chicks Hatch in Triumphant Recovery From Climate Disaster
stories.summary: Lake Tuz welcomed about 5,000 flamingo chicks in June, more than double last year's population, proving nature's recovery when given a chance.

stories.headline: Boulder Hiker Has Embroidered and Given Away Over 2,200 Caps on His Mountain—With No Plans to Stop
stories.summary: Curtis Jones spent his entire $72,000 retirement bonus on custom hats, giving away over 2,200 caps to hikers on Boulder's Mt. Sanitas.

stories.headline: Undergraduate Cracks Century-Old Math Problem, Could Reshape How Wind Turbines Generate Clean Power
stories.summary: Penn State student Divya Tyagi solves Hermann Glauert's hundred-year aerodynamics puzzle, unlocking vastly more efficient turbine design.

stories.headline: World's First Green Hydrogen Tanker Launches, Proving Clean Maritime Transport Is Here
stories.summary: The Rasa, a 42-meter tanker in Lithuania, becomes the world's first commercial vessel running entirely on green hydrogen and batteries, with zero diesel on board.

stories.headline: Nature's Gentle Undertakers Return: American Burying Beetles Fly Free in Ohio After 75 Years
stories.summary: Summary unchanged: excerpt does not contain information about American burying beetles or Ohio conservation efforts.
